status_notes: Source-level entries only, no article subpaths. See per-node urls for access status.
notes: Administers Japan's economic security legislation, including critical-infrastructure operator designation and the security-clearance system for economic-security-sensitive information.

match_nodes.description: Act on the Protection and Use of Critical Economic Security Information — security clearance system

data.official_name: Act on the Protection and Use of Critical Economic Security Information
data.notes: Act No. 27 of Reiwa 6 (2024), published May 2024, effective May 16, 2025. 8 chapters: I General Provisions, II Critical Economic Security Information (designation/scope), III Provision of Information, IV Management of Information, V Restrictions on Handlers, VI Suitability Assessment (security-clearance vetting, 10-year validity), VII Miscellaneous, VIII Penal Provisions. Establishes Japan's first formal security-clearance system for economic-security-sensitive government information — relevant to any cloud/vendor handling classified-adjacent economic-security data or seeking clearance-gated government contracts.

urls.note: Government-produced English OUTLINE only, not a full statutory translation. Returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch as of 2026-07-16.

match_nodes.description: Act on the Promotion of Ensuring National Security through Integrated Implementation of Economic Measures — critical infrastructure operator designation

data.official_name: Act on the Promotion of Ensuring National Security through Integrated Implementation of Economic Measures
data.notes: Act No. 43 of Reiwa 4 (2022), enacted May 2022, phased in through 2023-2024. Chapter I General Provisions, then 4 pillars: Ch. II stable supply of specified critical products (supply-chain resilience), Ch. III stable provision of specified critical infrastructure services (critical-infrastructure operator designation — 14-15 designated sectors incl. electricity, gas, water, rail, telecom, finance, credit cards; ~257 designated 'Specified Essential Infrastructure Service Providers' as of April 2026), Ch. IV support for critical/advanced technology development, Ch. V non-publication (secrecy) system for sensitive patent applications. Chapter III's designated operators must pre-notify and undergo government screening before introducing or outsourcing critical facilities/systems — a major supply-chain/vendor-risk control directly relevant to cloud providers serving Japanese critical infrastructure.

urls.note: Full English translation listed on the portal; returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch as of 2026-07-16.
Cabinet Office English overview PDF, not fetch-tested — separate domain from the blocked translation portal.
